excluded_defs.name: _deserializationContext
excluded_defs.reason: read: the ObjectMapper version of _deserializationContext passes mapper config directly; investigated to understand the design but not edited

exploration_log.aha_moment: The public forMapper() in the base class was called with the mapper config but immediately discarded it by not passing it to the abstract forMapper(). Overriding the public forMapper() in DefaultImpl to capture and store the config before constructing the cache was the key insight.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Just stores the config - the fix is upstream in how the config is selected before being stored

exploration_log.hindsight: Would have gone straight to the forMapper() override instead of spending time investigating whether ObjectReader._config could become stale.

alternatives: Change the abstract protected forMapper() signature
key_decisions.decision: Override public forMapper(Object,DeserializationConfig,TokenStreamFactory,DeserializerFactory) in DefaultImpl to store the config
key_decisions.reasoning: Overriding only in DefaultImpl is the least disruptive change; the base class signature stays stable for third-party subclasses

key_decisions.decision: Use null-check (config != null) ? config : _mapperConfig in createContext()
key_decisions.reasoning: Null-check makes the fallback path explicit and defends against cases where ObjectMapper.createContext() is called with mapper config directly

search_sequence.reasoning: Task mentions fixing ObjectReader._deserializationContext() as the primary site
search_sequence.result: Found method at line 2104 which calls _contexts.createContext(_config, _schema, _injectableValues)

search_sequence.reasoning: Task also mentions fixing the DeserializationContexts factory
search_sequence.result: Found DefaultImpl.createContext() at line 130 which already accepts a config parameter and passes it to DeserializationContextExt.Impl

search_sequence.action: Read ObjectReader constructors to understand _config lifecycle
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to confirm that _config in ObjectReader is the per-read config, not the mapper config
search_sequence.result: _config is set from the passed DeserializationConfig in all constructors; ObjectReader.without() creates a new reader with _config.without(feature)

search_sequence.action: Grep for forMapper calls in ObjectMapper.java
search_sequence.reasoning: Identified the gap: DefaultImpl discards the mapper config during forMapper, so it had no fallback
search_sequence.result: forMapper is called with _deserializationConfig (mapper-level config) during mapper initialization, but DefaultImpl doesn't store this config

search_sequence.reasoning: Confirmed the path from createContext config param to the final context
search_sequence.result: Simple pass-through constructor that stores config in parent DeserializationContext._config

queries.justification: Rule compliance: contains symbol names (createContext, DefaultImpl, _deserializationContext) and file paths. Pre-implementation: this is the query a developer would type after reading the task description.
queries.query_text: Fix ObjectReader._deserializationContext to pass per-read DeserializationConfig to DeserializationContexts.DefaultImpl.createContext so nested deserialization inherits feature overrides

solve_notes: The fix stores the mapper-level DeserializationConfig in DefaultImpl._mapperConfig by overriding the public forMapper() method, then makes createContext() use the passed per-read config (falling back to the stored mapper config only when null). ObjectReader._deserializationContext() already passes _config (the per-read config), so only comments were needed there to clarify intent.
task_text:
When calling `ObjectReader.readValue()` with per-read feature overrides
(e.g., `reader.without(DeserializationFeature.FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES)`),
the `DeserializationConfig` carrying those overrides must be passed to
`DeserializationContexts.DefaultImpl.createContext()`. If the wrong
config is supplied, nested objects deserialized during the same read
revert to mapper-level defaults. Fix the context construction in
`ObjectReader._deserializationContext()` and the `DeserializationContexts`
factory to carry forward per-read feature overrides into the created
`DeserializationContextExt` instance.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: forMapper
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the public base-class forMapper() is what receives the mapper config and calls the abstract forMapper(); understanding this call chain prevents an agent from incorrectly modifying the abstract method signature instead of overriding the public method

thrash_preventing_defs.name: _config
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms that _config in DeserializationContext is final and set from the constructor arg; this shows the config path ends here and must be correct at createContext() time

thrash_preventing_defs.name: _config
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows _config is the per-read config set from the constructor param; an agent needs to verify this before deciding whether ObjectReader or DeserializationContexts is the bug site

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Impl
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the concrete Impl class receiving the config from createContext(); checking its constructor confirms the config is forwarded to the parent without modification

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient covers the two edited sites. thrash_preventing adds the base-class forMapper() method (an agent might otherwise modify the abstract method signature or the wrong overload), the DeserializationContext._config field (confirms the config is stored final and must be correct before reaching the context), ObjectReader._config field (confirms it IS the per-read config, preventing the agent from thinking the fix must be in ObjectReader), and DeserializationContextExt.Impl (confirms the createContext output is correct as-is once the right config arrives).
